# Koji

> Documents in. Structured data out.

Koji is a self-hosted, config-driven document processing platform. It turns unstructured documents (PDFs, Word, images, scans) into clean, structured JSON data using local models or any API provider.

## Docs

- [Getting Started](https://docs.getkoji.dev/getting-started/): Install, start a cluster, process your first document
- [Configuration](https://docs.getkoji.dev/configuration/): Full koji.yaml reference — pipelines, models, clusters, output
- [Schemas](https://docs.getkoji.dev/schemas/): Define extraction schemas — field types, arrays, nested objects
- [Architecture](https://docs.getkoji.dev/architecture/): How the pipeline, services, and cluster model work
- [CLI Reference](https://docs.getkoji.dev/cli/): All commands — start, stop, process, status

## Quick Start

```bash
pip install koji-cli
koji start
koji process ./invoice.pdf --schema ./schemas/invoice.yaml
```

## Key Concepts

- **Pipeline**: Ordered steps defined in koji.yaml (parse → split → classify → extract → embed)
- **Schemas**: YAML files defining what data to extract (field names, types, descriptions)
- **Models**: BYO — local (ollama/vllm) or API (OpenAI, Anthropic, custom endpoints)
- **Cluster**: Docker-based services, one per pipeline step, orchestrated by a central server
- **CLI**: `koji start`, `koji stop`, `koji process`, `koji status`
